# WIRES — wires.org.uk

> WIRES (Web Infrastructure Rights for Everyone in Scotland) is a volunteer-led, independent, non-party campaign for affordable, reliable internet access as essential public infrastructure in Scotland. We hold government and institutions to account, support local groups across Scotland's 32 council areas, and speak directly to residents — not just to sector professionals.

## About WIRES

- About: https://wires.org.uk/about
- Why WIRES exists (landscape of organisations): https://wires.org.uk/landscape
- Organisational supporters: https://wires.org.uk/supporters

## The case for change

- Why home connectivity matters: https://wires.org.uk/why-it-matters
- Digital exclusion and health (WHO, COVID-19, NHS evidence): https://wires.org.uk/digital-health
- Beyond broadband — devices, design, authentication, skills, language: https://wires.org.uk/beyond-broadband

## Scotland

- Scotland policy and programmes: https://wires.org.uk/scotland
- Scottish connectivity stories (evidence-led): https://wires.org.uk/scotland-stories
- Council accountability tracker (who is acting on digital exclusion): https://wires.org.uk/accountability
- WiFi map by council area: https://wires.org.uk/wifi-map

## Help getting online

- Schemes and programmes for people in Scotland: https://wires.org.uk/get-help
- Write to your councillor (template letter): https://wires.org.uk/write-to-councillor
- Homelessness and digital exclusion: https://wires.org.uk/why-it-matters#homelessness

## Community and campaign

- Get involved: https://wires.org.uk/get-involved
- Local groups directory: https://wires.org.uk/groups
- Start a local group: https://wires.org.uk/start-a-group
- Global spotlight (community network models): https://wires.org.uk/global-spotlight

- Join as an individual: https://wires.org.uk/join
